title: Release Candidate Package
scientificQuestion: Can the bounded Open Verifiable Science OS v1 Candidate be packaged into a public, understandable, reusable evidence-routing package without overclaiming?
domain: Open Verifiable Science OS v1 Candidate packaging
limitations: This is a candidate package based on bounded route-policy-v3 workload evidence.
Passing classes are class-level candidates inside the measured workload, not a claim about all tasks.
Repo-package reproduction, formal counterexample, and temporal evaluation remain partial classes.
Public packages are minimum-evidence packages, not expert review reports.
The package has no outside-use evidence and no outside confirmation claim.
Time-to-evidence estimates are benchmark artifacts and require continued calibration.
disclaimer: Bounded OS v1 Candidate publication. It packages reusable route evidence and documentation; it does not claim broad acceleration, outside uptake, discovery validation, prize significance, broad autonomous intelligence, regulated professional authority, high-risk capability, or universal validity.
productChangeJustification: No Product code change was needed. The task was publication, documentation, corpus indexing, domain-pack packaging, and demo artifact creation around the already active route_policy_v3 capability.
